name: codex-project-governor
description: Initialize and govern Codex-ready projects with memory layers, iteration discipline, explicit project-scoped subagent activation, model routing, smart route guardrails, quality-gated acceleration, drift checks, upgrade advice, and scheduled memory compaction.

interface.shortDescription: Project initialization, memory, and governance for Codex
interface.longDescription: A Codex governance plugin that initializes repositories with project-scoped custom subagents, model-routing policy, smart task routing, context packs, pattern reuse, quality-gated subagent pipelines, route guard validation, repair loops, merge readiness, memory compaction, research gates, and upgrade advice.
defaultPrompt: Use Project Governor to initialize this existing repository without changing application code.
Use Project Governor to create a memory and iteration governance system for this project.
Use Project Governor to plan this change as an iteration, not a rewrite.
Use Project Governor to compact recent project activity into durable project memory.
Use Project Governor to advise whether project dependencies or tools should be upgraded before this change.
Use Project Governor to research a candidate capability, compare evidence, and recommend whether to adopt, spike, watch, or reject it before implementation.
Use Project Governor to research candidate release versions before upgrade advice, including skipped versions, evidence quality, relevance, and risk.
Use Project Governor to route this task into the fastest safe coding workflow with a change budget and quality level.
Use Project Governor to build a context pack and pattern reuse plan before implementation.
Use Project Governor to implement this feature through the quality-gated parallel feature builder.
Use Project Governor to run a quality gate, repair loop if needed, and merge-readiness review.
Use Project Governor to route simple explicit edits through micro_patch when safe.
Use Project Governor route-guard to verify the actual diff still fits the selected route.
Use Project Governor subagent-activation automatically when a routed task requires or benefits from parallel subagents.
Use project-scoped .codex/agents roles and model routing to balance speed and quality without requiring the user to specify subagents manually.
